annotations.plain-language-status: The headline tells you what's happening in your words, not the insurer's.
annotations.no-jargon-headlines: Intimation is a system word; the screen reads 'Claim registered' instead.
annotations.sla-with-owner-and-remedy: Every regulated deadline names who owes the response, and what your recourse is if they miss it.
annotations.bounded-not-precise: The time estimate says 'within 1 hour' — a bound the system can honour, not a countdown it will miss.
annotations.no-progress-without-data: The status bar only advances when an event is recorded; progress is earned, not animated.
annotations.nothing-to-do-is-a-design-choice: When the system is working, the explicit 'nothing required' affirmation prevents anxious checking.
annotations.waiting-with-ownership: The query screen names who asked, when they asked, and who must respond — not just 'under review'.
annotations.active-archetype-recedes-everything-else: When action is needed, the call-to-action is the only high-contrast element on screen.
annotations.statement-not-hero-metric: A settlement is a statement — claimed, approved, paid, deducted — not a single celebratory number.
annotations.indian-numerals-and-dates: Amounts use Indian lakh/crore grouping and dates use DD MMM YYYY, matching how patients read them.
annotations.grievance-as-recourse: The grievance route is rendered as the path forward, not as fine print after the fact.
annotations.deductions-itemised-not-summarised: Each deduction line names the rule that triggered it so the patient can dispute a specific item.
annotations.rejection-without-alarm-red: A rejection is rendered in neutral tones with next steps visible before the reason for rejection.
annotations.breach-is-clay-not-red: Breached deadlines read in clay/amber, not alarm-red. They're a recourse-trigger, not a disaster.
annotations.withdrawal-is-first-class: Withdrawal has its own terminal state and timeline entry — it isn't just a missing settlement.
annotations.replay-as-source-of-truth: The state you're looking at is computed from the event log, not stored. Every UI change is reproducible from the events.
annotations.events-not-guards: Transitions fire on explicit events, not on condition checks — so every state change has a named cause.
annotations.deterministic-interaction-ids: Each interaction ID is derived from its event sequence, so replays produce identical IDs without a database.
annotations.owner-colors-as-cross-component-motif: The same owner palette appears on the chip, the SLA bar, and the activity feed — one visual vocabulary.
